articles.title: Can Chinese AI solve inequality? + How dementia comes for your bank account
Trump heads to China amid ongoing war with Iran
articles.summary: President Trump's trip to China is going forward despite the war with Iran casting a shadow over the state visit.

articles.title: AI on the agenda as Trump heads to China
articles.summary: Artificial intelligence on the agenda for President Trump's upcoming meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping.

articles.title: New poll finds a majority of Americans unsure if attempts on Trump's life were real
articles.summary: When given the options of "true," "false" or "not sure," and asked whether each of the incidents "was staged," a majority of respondents said they thought each event was either staged or were unsure.

articles.title: Abortion pill by mail allowed for at least 3 more days, the Supreme Court says
articles.summary: Full access to the abortion pill mifepristone, including through telemedicine and the mail, will continue for at least three more days, the high court said on Monday.

articles.title: Reflecting Pool Repairs to Cost $13.1 Million. Trump Had Promised $1.8 Million.
articles.summary: The Interior Department added $6.2 million to the no-bid contract for repairs to the Lincoln Memorial pool late last week.

articles.title: Virginia Officials Ask Supreme Court to Restore Voting Map Drawn by Democrats
articles.summary: State officials asked the justices to overturn a Virginia Supreme Court decision that struck down a congressional map, a major defeat for Democrats.

articles.title: Supreme Court Continues Access to Abortion Pill by Mail, for a Few Days
articles.summary: Justice Samuel A. Alito Jr. has paused until at least Thursday a federal appeals court ruling against the Food and Drug Administration that would restrict access by mail to mifepristone.

articles.title: RFK Jr. Is Driving a Vast Inquiry Into Vaccines, Despite His Public Silence
articles.summary: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has toned down his public criticism of vaccines, under orders from the White House. But inside his department, a sprawling research effort is a top priority.

articles.title: The Voting Rights Decision Might Silence Black People in Fayette County, Tennessee
articles.summary: Black residents, who make up 25 percent of Fayette County, Tenn., won a new electoral map last year to change an all-white board of commissioners. They worry the Supreme Court’s ruling could threaten

articles.title: Trump rejects Iran's ceasefire proposal response. And, Congress to tackle ICE funding
articles.summary: Trump rejected Iran's response to a U.S. peace proposal, calling it "totally unacceptable." And, Congressional Republicans are trying to push for three years of funding for immigration enforcement.

articles.title: Trump Administration to Scrap Rule Encouraging Conservation
articles.summary: The Biden-era measure was intended to protect millions of acres from industrial development and the effects of climate change.

articles.title: Trump Proposes Suspending Federal Gas Tax Until Prices Fall
articles.summary: Congress would have to agree to any suspension, and dropping federal taxes would not make up for the steep increase in prices since the war began.

articles.title: Trump wants to suspend the federal gas tax as prices soar amid war with Iran
articles.summary: Suspending the federal gas tax would require an act of Congress.

articles.title: Modi Urges Indians to Buy Less Gold and Skip Foreign Trips as Iran War Strains Economy
articles.summary: Under pressure from the economic fallout of the war in Iran and politically emboldened, Prime Minister Narendra Modi asks Indians to sacrifice.

articles.title: 'Atlanta Journal-Constitution' chief steps down as bold goals yield to tough realities
articles.summary: The owners of the Atlanta Journal-Constitution invested $150 million to reinvent the paper. The changes have been significant. Three years in, the payoff has been modest.

articles.title: Oregon's most unexpected gubernatorial candidate? A pencil with a point
articles.summary: Oregon's public schools rank last in fourth-grade reading, according to an analysis of national testing. As a wake-up call for elected leaders, Pencil is running for governor as a write-in candidate.

articles.title: As ranks of uninsured grow, charity care can be hard to come by at many hospitals
articles.summary: An investigation of hospital data and charity care programs shows most Minnesota hospitals provide little financial aid to patients and often make assistance difficult to get.

articles.title: Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi transferred to a Tehran hospital, her foundation says
articles.summary: Nobel Peace laureate and activist Narges Mohammadi has been transferred to a Tehran hospital more than a week after collapsing in prison, her foundation said Sunday.

articles.title: Remains of a U.S. soldier who went missing in Morocco have been recovered
articles.summary: The remains of a U.S. soldier who went missing during military drills in Morocco were recovered in the Atlantic Ocean, the army said. Military teams are searching for a second missing soldier.

articles.title: U.S. cruise passengers arrive in the U.S. after one tests positive for hantavirus
articles.summary: One of the 18 Americans tested "mildly" positive for the virus during the U.S. return flight. A French woman has also tested positive — she was was among five French passengers repatriated to Paris.

articles.title: Israeli settlers force Palestinian family to exhume and rebury their father
articles.summary: The relatives of Hussein Asasa described to NPR how they were forced to exhume and rebury their father when Israeli settlers interfered with his grave.

articles.title: Trump says the Iran ceasefire is 'on life support' after rejecting an Iranian proposal
articles.summary: President Trump said Iran's latest response to a U.S. proposal was "unacceptable" and didn't address the country's nuclear program.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
